unit.name: SGD failure modes and momentum
unit.description: Explains vanilla SGD pathologies and how momentum uses velocity to address them.
unit.summary: Vanilla SGD can struggle when the loss changes quickly in one direction and slowly in another: it may overshoot or jitter along the steep direction while making slow progress along the shallow direction [ts=2318s]. This poor conditioning is connected to a large ratio between the Hessian's largest and smallest singular values [ts=2385s]. Another problem is local minima or saddle points, where zero or tiny gradients can stop gradient descent even though lower loss may be nearby [ts=2406s]. Saddle points become more common in high-dimensional models [ts=2487s]. A third problem is minibatch noise, because each SGD update estimates gradients from only a random subset [ts=2559s]. Momentum addresses these problems by building a velocity like a ball rolling down a hill, so previous gradients influence the next step and can smooth noise, pass through flat/saddle regions, and reduce oscillation [ts=2586s]. In the equations, SGD updates directly from the current gradient, while SGD+Momentum updates by velocity; rho controls how much past velocity is retained, often 0.9 or 0.99 [ts=2679s].

key_points.text: Poor conditioning makes SGD jitter in steep directions and progress slowly in shallow directions.
A too-large learning rate can overshoot and oscillate out of the valley.
Local minima and saddle points can have zero gradients, causing gradient descent to get stuck.
Saddle points are empirically more common in high-dimensional models.
Minibatch gradients are noisy because each update uses only a sampled subset of data.
Momentum builds a velocity or running average of gradients across iterations.
Momentum uses rho to weight past velocity versus current slope; typical rho values are 0.9 or 0.99.

kp_catalog.name: Gradient descent and stochastic gradient descent
kp_catalog.description: Neural networks are trained by computing the gradient of an objective and updating parameters with stochastic gradient descent. Gradient-based optimization updates parameters by subtracting a scaled gradient, with the learning rate controlling step size.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because gradient descent and stochastic gradient descent formalizes how neural networks are trained by computing the gradient of an objective and updating parameters with stochastic gradient descent, which later units use as core machinery rather than background context.

kp_catalog.name: SGD failure modes
kp_catalog.description: Poor conditioning, overshoot, local minima, saddle points, and minibatch gradient noise.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because sGD failure modes clarifies poor conditioning, overshoot, local minima, saddle points, and minibatch gradient noise, sharpening later modeling, analysis, or evaluation choices.

kp_catalog.name: Momentum optimizer
kp_catalog.description: Using velocity or a running average of gradients to smooth updates and keep moving in useful directions.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because momentum optimizer clarifies using velocity or a running average of gradients to smooth updates and keep moving in useful directions, sharpening later modeling, analysis, or evaluation choices.
